description: A gain module with decibel range and parameter smoothing

parameterGroups.label: Gain
parameterGroups.rationale: Gain sets the target amplitude; Smoothing controls transition time to avoid clicks

parameterGroups.label: Voice reset
parameterGroups.rationale: ResetValue sets the gain value applied when a voice starts (before smoothing ramps to the target)

gaps.question: Does core.gain multiply all channels by the same gain value? Is it block-based (using sfloat/sdouble smoothing) or frame-based?
gaps.context: Users need to know whether gain is applied per-sample with smoothing or per-block.

gaps.question: The Gain parameter is in decibels (-100 to 0 dB). Is -100 dB treated as silence (0.0 linear) or as the actual -100 dB value?
gaps.context: Important for users who want true silence at the minimum setting.

gaps.question: When exactly is ResetValue applied? On voice start (reset() callback)? Does it jump immediately to ResetValue and then smooth to the current Gain?
gaps.context: Users need to know how ResetValue interacts with polyphonic voice lifecycle.

gaps.question: What smoothing algorithm is used -- sfloat (exponential ramp) or linear ramp? Is the 20ms default suitable for click-free gain changes?
gaps.context: Helps users understand audio quality implications of the smoothing time setting.

notes: core.gain is one of the most fundamental and frequently used scriptnode nodes. The decibel range (-100 to 0) means it can only attenuate, not amplify. The high skew value (5.42) creates a logarithmic feel on the parameter slider. IsPolyphonic but not IsProcessingHiseEvent -- gain is per-voice but not MIDI-driven. ResetValue likely handles the common case where gain should start at a specific value on voice start.
